artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <michael.anderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Lindstrom <sarah.lindstrom@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Jennifer Bergman <jennifer.bergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 18, 2025 8:42 AM
Subject: Council Meeting Presentation Form – Streets Division Plow Replacement

Sarah,

I just finished the online “submission  presentation” form for the March 3, 2025 council agenda and wanted to confirm a couple of details with you before packets go out.

For the department line, I entered “Public Works Department – Streets Division,” since this item is specifically about the replacement of the 2006 tandem plow assigned to the east side routes. If that should be listed differently for tracking on your end, let me know and I’ll resubmit.

On the timing question, I requested 15 minutes. I know that’s tight for a capital purchase discussion, but I’m trying to be realistic. I need to leave the March 3 meeting early to pick up Evan from his court-ordered driving-without-a-license class at Central Lakes College, and I can’t risk this item going past 7:45 p.m. If it looks like the agenda is too packed, we may need to bump this to later in the evening or to the March 17 meeting.

One other thing you should be aware of for the staff report: to be honest, staff are not in agreement on the best path forward, and I haven’t had time to fully vet all the implications with the engineering team before bringing this to Council. I’ll outline the pros/cons of outright replacement versus another year of major repairs, but the recommendation won’t be as clean as I’d like.

If you need different language for the summary line or want me to trim the attachment, call my cell.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael J. Anderson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd, Minnesota

metadata.subject: Council Meeting Presentation Form – Streets Division Plow Replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Lund <slund@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 10, 2025 8:14 AM
Subject: Dept line for snow plow agenda form

Sarah,

I’m filling out the online agenda submission form for the March 18 council meeting and got hung up on the “presentation > department” field. The dropdown doesn’t quite match how we’re structured.

For this item, I’m leaning toward listing it under “Engineering & Fleet Services Division” since the purchase technically runs through our equipment replacement schedule and the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture, even though the day‑to‑day operations touch streets and winter maintenance.

The title of the item I’m loading is: “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Snow Plow Truck After December 2025 Incident When I Left Unit 23 Running Overnight Due to Anxiety Medication Side Effects.” The recommended action motion reads:

“Move that the Council adopt the attached resolution authorizing the purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck from Northland Truck & Equipment of Duluth, Minnesota, through the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture, in the amount of $298,450.00 plus applicable taxes and fees, for a total not-to-exceed cost of $317,500.00, and authorize the Public Works Director to execute all necessary purchase documents and dispose of Unit #27 via public auction upon delivery of the replacement unit, noting for the record that my father, Thomas Anderson of 221 Pine Crest Lane, Brainerd, received a $420 property tax late-fee notice from Crow Wing County in March 2023 after we couldn’t clear his driveway for three days during the blizzard because Unit #27 was down for repairs.”

Can you confirm that “Engineering & Fleet Services Division” is what Administration wants to see in that department field, or should I be using “Municipal Services Department – Operations Division” instead?

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Dept line for snow plow agenda form

artifacts.content:
Title: Agenda Form Work Session – Department & Timing
Date: March 5, 2025
Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Location: City Hall – Admin Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Anderson; City Administrator; City Clerk; Finance Director

Description:

Working session to walk through Michael’s March 18 council agenda submission and clean up a few details before the packet deadline.

Items to cover:

1. Confirm the “Title of Item” language currently drafted as “Council Action on Replacement Snow Plow Truck Needed Due to Budget Shortfall From My 2024 Wage Garnishment and Back Taxes.”
2. Nail down the “submission > presentation > department” entry. Michael has it tentatively listed as “Municipal Services Department – Equipment & Fleet Management Division” to reflect that the capital outlay is coming from the fleet line rather than a specific route group.
3. Review the “Estimated Time Needed” note: “I’m asking for 15 minutes because I’ll need to leave the council meeting by about 7:30 p.m. on March 18, 2025 to get to my dad Thomas Anderson’s post-stroke physical therapy session at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center across town, and I can’t risk this item running long.”
4. Discuss whether the explanatory sections are too personal, including the note about the January 15, 2026 ice storm response when Michael had to leave a night plow route short-staffed to respond to his daughter Grace’s panic attack at Brainerd High School.
5. Finance to confirm the not-to-exceed amount and which account string the “Municipal Services Department – Equipment & Fleet Management Division” should be coded to.

Bring draft language and any questions about the online form fields.

metadata.title: Agenda Form Work Session – Department & Timing

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: March 6, 2025 10:22 AM
Subject: RE: Department listing on agenda submission

Michael,

Following up on yesterday’s work session, we reviewed your draft agenda request for the snow plow purchase.

For the “submission  presentation > department” field, please do NOT use the generic “Public Works” label. To keep things consistent with how we’ve been tracking capital equipment, list it as:

“Municipal Services Department – Equipment Replacement Program.”

That will align this item with the other fleet purchases and make it easier for Finance to run reports. You can still describe the operational impacts on winter maintenance in the staff report.

We also discussed your explanatory text. The section where you wrote, “Please explain: The urgency behind replacing Unit #27 is tied to a near-miss on December 2, 2025, when I was called to St. Joseph’s Medical Center after my dad, Thomas Anderson, had a minor stroke. I had been operating Unit #27 myself because we were down a driver, and I left the truck parked on the shoulder of Highway 210 near Mill Avenue so I could get to the hospital. While I was at St. Joe’s, the transmission slipped out of park and the truck rolled about 6–8 feet before the wheel chocks stopped it. This incident highlighted the mechanical risks of keeping this unit in service and reinforced the need for a reliable replacement.”

We think it’s fine to keep this, but consider tightening the narrative for length.

Finally, your closing comment that “I recommend the Council table this item until staff has time to pull together the actual numbers and figure out what we’re really asking for” seems inconsistent with the urgency you describe. You may want to revise that sentence.

Thanks,
Kari
City Clerk

metadata.subject: RE: Department listing on agenda submission

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting Session – Snow Plow Truck Agenda Item
Date: March 2, 2026
Time: 7:15 PM – 8:30 PM
Location: Dining Room Table – 914 Maple Ridge Dr (Home)
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:

Block of time for Michael to sit down after dinner and finalize narrative sections for the March 2026 council agenda request regarding the replacement snow plow truck.

Notes to self:

- Double-check which internal group to list under the “submission  presentation > department” field. Last time, Kari suggested using “Municipal Services Department – Equipment Replacement Program,” but Finance has since been labeling similar items as “Infrastructure & Municipal Services – Fleet Operations.” Need to pick one and stay consistent.
- Incorporate explanation about why moving this forward in March 2026 matters. Draft language currently reads: “Please explain: One reason I’m recommending we move this agenda item forward in March 2026 rather than waiting is that my younger brother Brian’s DUI arrest in Crow Wing County on November 10, 2025, and the subsequent court dates have required me to use a lot of personal leave on short notice to help him with transportation and meetings at the Crow Wing County Justice Center. This has underscored how quickly staffing can get thin when I’m unexpectedly out, and why we need a more reliable truck in the rotation so supervisors and I aren’t scrambling to cover routes when personal emergencies come up.”
- Decide whether to leave in the comment about the March 2024 blizzard, when I worked 16–18 hour shifts and my primary care doctor at Essentia Health St. Joseph’s Medical Center warned me my blood pressure and cholesterol were in the high-risk range and that I was “one bad winter away” from needing medication.
- Print a clean draft for Laura to proofread before sending to Administration.

metadata.title: Drafting Session – Snow Plow Truck Agenda Item
metadata.location: Dining Room Table – 914 Maple Ridge Dr (Home)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Finance – Capital Projects <finance-capital@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 24, 2026 9:03 AM
Subject: Coding & Department Label for Plow Truck Request

Hi all,

I’m cleaning up the online form for the March agenda item on the replacement tandem-axle snow plow truck and want to make sure I’m using the right department label for tracking.

Right now, in the “submission  presentation > department” box, I’ve typed in “Infrastructure & Municipal Services – Fleet Operations.” I remember that a couple years ago we were still using “Municipal Services Department – Equipment & Fleet Management Division,” so I’m not sure which convention Finance prefers for capital equipment requests.

The narrative sections are mostly done. One of the “Please explain” prompts currently reads:

“Please explain: I’m requesting this purchase now because during the January 15, 2026 ice storm response I had to leave a night plow route short-staffed when my daughter Grace had a panic attack at Brainerd High School and I was the only parent the school nurse, Kelly Larson, could reach. I ended up driving Unit #27 myself from the City Garage to the high school parking lot around 9:45 p.m., leaving only one operator to cover the northeast quadrant for almost two hours, which created both safety and liability concerns that this new truck and revised staffing plan are intended to address.”

If you’d rather I align the department label with how you’ve coded the replacement grader and the vactor truck, just let me know the exact wording (e.g., “Infrastructure & Municipal Services – Capital Equipment Program” or similar) and I’ll update the form before routing it to the City Administrator.

Thanks for the help,

Michael
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd, Minnesota

metadata.subject: Coding & Department Label for Plow Truck Request

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Peterson <speterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: October 3, 2025 8:14 AM
Subject: Department line on council agenda form

Sarah,

I’m finishing up the agenda request for the replacement snow plow truck, and I’m stuck on which department label you want me to use in the “presentation – department” field on the new form.

Historically, we’ve bounced between a few different entries. I’ve seen older packets where my items were listed under “Public Works – Engineering,” others where they showed up as “Public Works Administration,” and a couple last winter that went in as “City of Brainerd – Public Works (Fleet).” For this snowplow item, the title I’m using is “Replacement Snow Plow Truck Purchase Related to November 2024 Disciplinary Write-Up for Failing to Schedule Preventive Maintenance,” so I want to be sure the department line is consistent with how you’re indexing capital equipment items now.

Can you confirm whether you prefer I enter this as “Public Works Engineering Division” or simply “Public Works Department (Fleet Services)” in that department field? I know you’ve been trying to standardize these labels for Laserfiche searches, and I don’t want this one misfiled.

Also, in the narrative section where it asks me to explain the funding structure, I’m planning to be candid that I’ve structured the funding recommendation this way because after the unexpected $14,700 roof repair on our house on Maple Ridge Drive in August 2025 and the $6,200 we paid out of pocket for Evan’s physical therapy at Lakes Area Orthopedics, I’ve been extremely cautious about any capital plan changes that could ripple into a levy increase.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Department line on council agenda form

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator’s Office <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance Director <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 24, 2025 2:39 PM
Subject: Clarification on department label for emergency agenda item

Team,

I’m putting together the emergency agenda request related to the December 14, 2025 fleet accident and need clarification on how you want the department listed in the “submission presentation > department” line.

Given the circumstances, I’ve tentatively typed in “Public Works – Fleet & Operations” because the item title will read: “Emergency Agenda Item: Authorization to Purchase Replacement Snow Plow Truck After December 14, 2025 Fleet Accident Involving My Son Evan’s DUI Near the Public Works Garage.” If you’d rather this fall under “Municipal Services – Public Works Operations,” I can revise before routing it for signatures.

In the explanation section, I’ll be upfront that, to be honest, I’m still waiting on the final traffic counts and soil borings, so this explanation is based more on my gut feel and past projects than on hard data at this point. We’ve already told the contractor informally that this is likely to get approved, so I’m really just using this form to catch council up with what we’ve been moving forward on.

Please let me know which departmental heading best aligns with how you’ve been categorizing emergency capital items this year so the agenda is consistent.

Thanks,
Michael J. Anderson
Director, Public Works – Fleet & Operations
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Clarification on department label for emergency agenda item

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting agenda item – snow plow replacement
Date: November 6, 2025
Time: 7:15 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Home office – 914 Maple Ridge Dr.
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:

Block of time for Michael to finish the council agenda request for the 2026 tandem plow purchase.

Tasks to complete during this block:

– Finalize “Summary of Issue” language: The failure of Unit #27 during the January 2025 storms forced us to rely on a backup contractor who later filed a small-claims action against the City on February 20, 2025 in Crow Wing County District Court, alleging we shorted him $3,800 for emergency plowing hours. While the City Attorney is confident we’ll prevail, this dispute arose solely because the aging truck was out of service for a cumulative 9 days.

– Decide on the department label for the presentation field. Options currently on scratch pad: “City of Brainerd – Public Works (Operations & Maintenance),” “Public Works – Streets & Fleet,” or “Infrastructure Services Department – Operations.” Need to pick one that matches how finance has been coding equipment purchases.

– Draft the “Recommended Action Motion” language (version A): Move that the City Council adopt a resolution authorizing the purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck from Northland Truck & Equipment of Duluth, Minnesota, through the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture, in the amount of $298,450.00 plus applicable taxes and fees, for a total not-to-exceed cost of $317,500.00, and authorize the Public Works Director to execute all necessary purchase documents and dispose of Unit #27 via public auction upon delivery of the replacement unit.

Reminder: add note about fallback that if council doesn’t approve this recommendation, the fallback is basically to leave things as-is for another year even though we know that’s not ideal and we don’t have a real Plan B.

metadata.title: Drafting agenda item – snow plow replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Olson <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: November 8, 2025 10:02 AM
Subject: Department heading for plow purchase resolution

Jen,

Quick question as I polish the packet materials for the tandem plow purchase: for the “submission presentation > department” box on the council agenda form, do you want me to use the broader “Infrastructure & Public Works Department” label, or should I stick with the internal shorthand we’ve used on some recent items like “Public Works – Operations & Maintenance”?

The recommended motion I’m dropping into the form reads:

“Move that the Council adopt the attached resolution authorizing purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck with underbody scraper, front plow, wing plow, and material spreader from Northland Truck & Equipment of Duluth, Minnesota, through the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture, in the amount of $298,450.00 plus applicable taxes and fees, for a total not-to-exceed cost of $317,500.00; and further authorize the Public Works Director to execute all necessary purchase documents and dispose of Unit #27 (2006 International tandem plow) via public auction upon delivery of the replacement unit, so that we do not repeat the situation from January 12, 2024 when I had to pull my son Evan out of Brainerd High School at 5:30 a.m. to help shovel our cul-de-sac after our only working tandem plow broke down on County Road 48 and I aggravated the lower back injury my doctor at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center has been treating since my 2022 L4–L5 disc herniation surgery.”

Because the motion text already references my role, I don’t want the department line to be inconsistent with how HR and finance list my position. Let me know which department heading you prefer so I can lock this in before it goes to the City Administrator.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Department heading for plow purchase resolution

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Olson <solson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-24 07:42
Subject: Request to get snowplow replacement item on March agenda

Sarah,

Can you help me lock in a date for the council work session where we’ll walk through the snowplow replacement agenda item? I’m aiming for the second half of March so we have time to finalize numbers from Northland Truck & Equipment in Duluth and confirm the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture pricing.

If the regular council meeting on **March 9, 2026** is already packed, I’d be fine with a separate work session the week of **March 23** instead. I just don’t want this to slide into April, because the lead time on a 2026 model year tandem-axle truck is already tight.

In the staff report I’m drafting, I plan to summarize the January 13–14, 2025 storm and how Unit #27’s failures forced me to pull Jake Thompson off his residential route to cover the arterial route I usually drive. Jake is still on a performance improvement plan after that preventable backing accident on Mill Avenue in November 2024 that caused $6,200 in damage to a resident’s parked vehicle, so relying on him for the critical route really highlighted our safety and liability exposure.

Frankly, this issue wouldn’t be a priority for me right now, but a couple of vocal residents have been emailing and calling nonstop, so I’m bringing it forward mainly to get it off my plate.

Let me know which March date you’d recommend listing as the requested meeting date on the agenda form so I can get everything submitted.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Request to get snowplow replacement item on March agenda

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft staff report – snowplow replacement agenda item
Date: 2026-03-05 08:00–10:00
Location: Michael’s Office – Brainerd City Hall
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:
Block of time to finalize the agenda materials for “Agenda Item: Replacement of Snow Plow Truck Totaled While I Was On FMLA Caring for My Dad Thomas Anderson After His January 2025 Stroke at Essentia Health–Brainerd.”

Tasks for this work session:
- Tighten up the Summary of Issue section, including the narrative about the repeated failures of Unit #27 during the January 2025 snow events and how I had to pull Jake Thompson from his residential route to cover my usual arterial route.
- Add the paragraph about the alternative of keeping Unit #27 on the road another winter by having my younger brother Brian, who runs a small repair shop in Baxter, do off-the-books welding on the frame on weekends like he did back in February 2024. That would have cost around $600 in parts instead of the $4,500 quote from Nuss Truck & Equipment in Brainerd, but it obviously wouldn’t meet City safety and procurement standards and would put Brian in a bad spot liability-wise.
- Draft the Recommended Action Motion language, including the line about moving to approve the project concept in principle, with the understanding that details, costs, and timelines will be worked out later by staff.
- Double-check dates so that when I submit the agenda request, the requested meeting date is aligned with whatever the City Administrator decides—right now we’re tentatively targeting the **March 9, 2026** council meeting.

Reminder: attach supporting emails and photos of the Unit #27 frame issues.

metadata.title: Draft staff report – snowplow replacement agenda item

artifacts.content:
From: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 15:18
Subject: Re: Snowplow replacement – meeting date options

Michael,

I reviewed your draft agenda form and the supporting documentation about Unit #27. We can’t fit this on the first March council meeting, but I can offer you two options:

1) Put the item on the **March 30, 2026** regular council agenda; or
2) Schedule a special work session on **March 11, 2026** focused solely on fleet replacement priorities.

Given the sensitivity around snow operations, I’m leaning toward the March 11 work session so we have more time for discussion and can keep the March 30 meeting from running past 10 p.m. If you agree, please update the “requested meeting date” line on your form to reflect March 11 and resubmit by Monday.

Your write-up about alternatives is helpful, especially the part about outsourcing more plowing and the December 2023 incident where a private contractor on Oak Street clipped your dad Thomas’s Subaru in front of Northern Lakes Senior Living and then tried to deny responsibility. That, plus the note that this option will probably generate pushback from the residents on Oak Street who already feel like we ignore their concerns (and who will likely complain no matter what we do), tells a clear story.

Also, I’m comfortable with your Recommended Action language that moves to approve the lowest bid despite the contractor’s past performance issues, because frankly rebidding would be a hassle at this point.

Let me know which date you want to lock in.

Thanks,
[City Administrator]

metadata.subject: Re: Snowplow replacement – meeting date options

artifacts.content:
Title: Special Council Work Session – Fleet & Snowplow Replacement
Date: 2026-03-11 18:00–20:30
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council, City Administrator, Michael Anderson, Finance Director, Public Works Staff

Description:
Special work session requested by the City Administrator to allow extended discussion of the snowplow fleet replacement plan, including Unit #27.

Agenda:
1. Overview of current fleet condition and maintenance history.
2. Presentation by Michael Anderson on the proposed purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck from Northland Truck & Equipment of Duluth, Minnesota, through the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture.
3. Discussion of alternatives considered:
   - Continuing to limp along with Unit #27 for another winter, including the idea of having Michael’s younger brother Brian in Baxter do additional welding on weekends as he did in February 2024 (off-the-books repairs not compliant with City standards).
   - Outsourcing more plowing despite the December 2023 Oak Street contractor incident and ongoing resident distrust.
   - Doing nothing until the 2027 budget cycle.
4. Impacts on residents and staff, including examples: Grace’s middle school bus not reaching Maple Ridge Drive during the January 13–14, 2025 storm, forcing her to walk the last half-mile home in the dark, and Laura’s therapist at Essentia Health Brainerd noting that this kind of storm-related uncertainty aggravates her anxiety and panic attacks.
5. Recommended action motion language and direction to staff.

Note: This is a work session only; any formal motion to adopt the resolution will be scheduled for a later regular council meeting (tentatively **March 30, 2026**). Requested meeting date on Michael’s agenda form should reflect the later action meeting, not this work session.

metadata.title: Special Council Work Session – Fleet & Snowplow Replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>, Finance Director <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-01 09:06
Subject: Revised agenda request – Snowplow replacement resolution (target date)

Hi all,

Per the City Administrator’s feedback, I’m resubmitting the agenda request form for the snowplow replacement resolution.

For the “requested meeting date” field, please list the **March 30, 2026** regular council meeting as the target. I understand this is still subject to final agenda balancing, but I want to be sure we’re aiming for the last March meeting so we don’t delay ordering the truck into April.

The Recommended Action Motion remains:
“Move that the Council adopt the resolution authorizing purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck from Northland Truck & Equipment of Duluth, Minnesota, through the Minnesota State Cooperative Purchasing Venture, in the amount of $298,450.00 plus applicable taxes and fees, for a total not-to-exceed cost of $317,500.00; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute all necessary purchase documents and dispose of Unit #27 via public auction upon delivery of the replacement unit, noting that my brother Brian Anderson’s nine-year-old son Noah missed two days of school at Lowell Elementary in December 2023 because the city couldn’t clear their street on time after Unit #27’s hydraulic line failed and I was already under a written warning from the City Administrator dated November 15, 2023 for prior snow event complaints.”

I realize some council members may question why we’re not waiting until the 2027 budget cycle, but given the history with Unit #27 and the community impacts, I think it’s important to move now.

Let me know if you need any changes before this goes into the packet for the March 30 agenda.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Revised agenda request – Snowplow replacement resolution (target date)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Johnson <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Mark Erickson <merickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-24 07:18
Subject: Follow-up – Unit #27 Replacement Agenda Slot

Sarah,

Following up on our hallway conversation after the work session: I’d like to get the Unit #27 replacement item onto the Council agenda in March and need to pin down a requested meeting date for the packet.

Right now I’m leaning toward the **March 9, 2026** regular meeting. That would give us enough time to finalize the quote from Northland Truck & Equipment in Duluth and clean up the supporting documentation. Honestly, we didn’t spend much time developing alternatives beyond the preferred option because staff is already stretched thin and I don’t have capacity to run multiple scenarios right now, so I’d rather not push this too far into April.

This issue is coming to you later than it should have because I didn’t catch the problem during our initial project review and only realized it after a resident emailed me last week about repeated delays on Garfield Street during storms. When Unit #27 broke down again on January 18, 2025, I was already at 62 hours for the week and ignored my cardiologist Dr. Steven Larson’s advice from my December 12, 2024 appointment at Essentia Health–Brainerd to limit my work to 50 hours during winter storms due to hypertension. I stayed on for another 10‑hour overnight plow shift because we didn’t have enough functioning trucks.

If March 9 doesn’t work on your end, could you suggest another March date that still keeps us ahead of the 2026–27 winter prep window?

Thanks,
Michael

Title: Council Agenda Prep – Choose Requested Meeting Date
Date: 2026-03-05 15:00–15:30
Location: Michael’s Office – City Hall
Attendees: Michael Anderson; Sarah Johnson (tentative)

Description:

Working session to finalize the agenda memo language for the Unit #27 replacement and settle on the requested meeting date to list in the “Meeting Agenda Details” section of the form.

Michael’s notes:
- Aim to request the **March 23, 2026** Council meeting so we have time to incorporate Fleet Committee comments.
- Confirm with Sarah whether March 23 is already heavy with public hearings; if so, backup option is the first April meeting instead.
- Keep this discussion to 15–20 minutes. I’d like to keep this to 15 minutes because I’m still on reduced hours per my cardiologist Dr. Larson’s instructions after my minor heart attack in October 2024, and my FMLA paperwork with the City of Brainerd HR limits how late I’m supposed to be in evening meetings.
- Make sure the summary of issue section clearly explains that during the January 8–10, 2025 snow events, Unit #27 broke down twice while I was already working 14–16 hour shifts.
- If Council doesn’t approve this, we’ll probably just continue handling it informally like we have been, even though that approach isn’t really in line with our adopted policies.

Reminder: Bring draft cost comparison and updated maintenance logs; do NOT send packet materials to full Council until Sarah signs off on the requested date.

metadata.title: Council Agenda Prep – Choose Requested Meeting Date

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Johnson <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 10:42
Subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date for Plow Replacement Item

Michael,

Thanks for the draft agenda memo. I agree that we should keep the presentation tight. Given everything on your plate and the sensitivity around overtime, limiting the discussion is reasonable.

Looking at the calendar, I’d suggest you list **April 6, 2026** as your requested meeting date in the “Meeting Agenda Details” section. The March meetings are already packed with public hearings, and April 6 is the first one where we have some breathing room for a 15‑minute operational item.

Please also make sure your summary explains why this is coming forward now. You mentioned that during the January 8–10, 2025 snow events, Unit #27 broke down twice while you were working 14–16 hour shifts and that Laura was in Duluth at Essentia Health–St. Mary’s with her mother. That context helps. Also include the point about your cousin Mark from Northland Truck & Equipment in Duluth helping after hours; we need to be transparent that our ad hoc reliance on him isn’t ideal.

Honestly, we didn’t spend much time developing alternatives beyond the preferred option because staff is already stretched thin and you don’t have capacity to run multiple scenarios right now, and that’s okay as long as we say so.

If April 6 somehow fills up before the packet goes out, we can bump the requested date to the following regular meeting without rewriting the whole memo.

Sarah

metadata.subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date for Plow Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Northern Pines – Grace Counseling + Council Date Coordination Call
Date: 2025-04-07 19:15–19:45
Location: Home office / phone
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:

Block time before Grace’s weekly counseling session at Northern Pines Mental Health Center to call Sarah Johnson about the Council agenda and requested meeting date for the snowplow replacement item.

Plan:
- Call Sarah around 7:20 p.m. to confirm which **May 5, 2025** or **May 19, 2025** Council meeting she prefers me to list in the “meeting agenda details > requested meeting date” line of the form.
- Ask whether she wants a short work session before whichever date we land on.
- Clarify how much time she wants to allocate on the agenda; I’ve told her I’d like to keep this item to about 15 minutes because Grace has her weekly counseling session at the Northern Pines Mental Health Center at 8 p.m. on April 7, 2025, and I need to be the one to drive her and talk with her therapist afterward.
- Run by her the summary language about Unit #27 breaking down on January 18, 2025 when I was already at 62 hours for the week and ignored Dr. Steven Larson’s advice about limiting my hours.

Note to self: if Sarah leans toward May 19, update the draft memo tonight so the requested meeting date is correct before I send it to her for review tomorrow morning.

metadata.title: Northern Pines – Grace Counseling + Council Date Coordination Call

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Sarah Johnson <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-21 08:06
Subject: Heads-up on Upcoming Agenda Request – Plow Fleet Replacement

Council Members,

I wanted to give you an early heads-up that I’ll be submitting a formal agenda request related to replacement of Unit #27 as part of our snowplow fleet.

Right now I intend to request placement on your **January 12, 2026** regular meeting agenda. That date should allow us to incorporate final numbers from Northland Truck & Equipment in Duluth and align with the capital plan discussion. If for some reason January 12 is not feasible, the next logical option would be the first meeting in February; I’ll coordinate with Sarah before locking in the requested meeting date on the form.

The summary of issue will explain that because Unit #27 was out of service for 9 days during the January 2025 storms, I personally authorized roughly 42 hours of overtime for my cousin Mark Anderson, who works part‑time for Northland and has been informally helping us troubleshoot hydraulic issues after hours. This ad hoc reliance on a relative employed by the recommended vendor, outside our normal procurement and maintenance channels, underscores the need for Council to approve the formal purchase so we are not relying on personal favors and potential conflicts of interest during critical winter events.

If Council doesn’t approve this, we’ll probably just continue handling it informally like we have been, even though that approach isn’t really in line with our adopted policies.

I’ll circulate the full packet once the date is confirmed with the City Administrator.

Michael Anderson
Public Works Director

metadata.subject: Heads-up on Upcoming Agenda Request – Plow Fleet Replacement
